text: in front of her as she carefully set down her bare foot. “You’re not going to — ” Ronin closed the distance between them in two strides, dipped into a crouch, and swept a forearm behind her knees . “What the hell are you doing? I said no!” she shouted as he stood upright and held her against his chest. She twisted and thrashed to escape his hold, but her struggles only resulted in her being tangled in her wet clothing . “You made an agreement with me. You can’t listen to my proposal if you get killed on the way back .” “Even if I get an infection, it’s not going to kill me right away!” She pounded on his chest three times and then shook her hand, wincing. “I’ve done well enough on my own, so far. I can manage a mile walk home .” “Probably could. I’m just making sure of it, so I get my end of the deal .” “I haven’t even gotten mine yet,” she said, brows angled down over the bridge of her nose. She clutched her damaged boot to her chest and turned her head and shoulders as far away from him as she could . “Soon.” Ronin walked, increasing his pace steadily as he passed the deepest floodwater. Though the air had cooled significantly with the storm and wind, the woman’s body was hot against his. He shifted from a slow jog to a run . “What are you doing?” she asked . “ Hold on .” Chapter Five L ara was pissed by the time they reached her home. She didn’t wait for the bot to stop before leaping out of its arms, splashing mud all over her legs when she landed. The toes of her bare foot sank in the muck, but she marched on, refusing to acknowledge her discomfort. Water poured over the edge of the roof to land on her already drenched head when she stopped at the door . Swearing under her breath, she shoved the door aside and stomped into the shack. She threw her broken boot into a corner, dropped her bag on the pallet, and spun on the bot, thrusting a finger at it before it entered . “Don’t you ever do that again, you son of a bitch .” “Not the son of anything .” “Don’t get smart with me. You had no right handling me that way .” “I have every right to ensure our agreement is fulfilled .” “We don’t have an ag—What are you doing?” She retreated to maintain the distance between them as it walked inside. “I didn’t invite you in !” “I’m not going to stand outside, in the rain, while I make my offer .” She glared at it, gaze moving from its face to trek over its body. The way it looked and moved, it was so damned human . She’d even felt its warmth through their clothing while it held her. If it weren’t for its metal hands, she would never have guessed its true nature . Lara reminded herself of the key difference. Most humans killed for good reason — survival. But these things killed because they were stronger; they killed because they could . Bots didn’t have a conscience. It didn’t matter if this one had voiced concern for her, had offered to bandage the scrape on her knee, had carried her home so she didn’t injure herself further … She leaned down, pulled off her intact boot, and tossed it near its mate. Not without hesitation, she turned her back on the bot and crossed the space to the lantern. It took several frustrating attempts to produce a flame with the lighter, but the soft, familiar glow was a relief. Inhaling deeply, she tucked the lighter away and returned her attention to the bot . “Shit!” She started, hand flying to her chest. The bot stood directly behind her. She hadn’t even heard it move . It was silent, eyes on her. The intensity of its unblinking stare reminded her again how different they were. How dangerous it was . Glancing down, she noticed it was holding out the bundle of food. Hunger decided to reintroduce itself in that moment, churning her stomach. She snatched the food from the bot’s hand . “I’m not going to say thank you,” she said around a mouthful of meat . The burst of flavor had her salivating. She took another bite before she finished the first . “You don’t have to. Just listen .” “Lihshing.” So. Damn . Good . When the bot said no more, Lara looked up at it. It still had its watchful stare fixed on her. The rain drummed on the roof, the sound competing with that of her chewing . “What?” she asked, wadding the food in one cheek . “When was the last time you ate ?” “ Why ?” “If it’s been a while, you’ll make yourself sick .” “Screw you. I don’t tell you where to shove your oil, or whatever. I’ll eat how I want.” She took another bite, chewing with her mouth open to display the food. If the bot said a single damned thing about her manners, she’d spit the meat right in its face. It’d be worth the waste of food . Almost . The bot stared for a little longer, and, finally, turned away. “I’ll wait until you’re done .” Lara scoffed and moved to her pallet. Just before her ass settled atop it, she remembered that she was soaked. Scowling, she straightened back up and continued to eat, savoring the flavor despite her ravenousness . She hadn’t eaten in two days . With nothing else to look at, her eyes wandered to the bot. Its attention was on her collection of treasures. Without its penetrating stare upon her, she was free to study it . It wore a faded olive coat, repaired in more places than she could count by
